initiatives.title: K-State Responsible Use of Generative AI Task Force
initiatives.description: K-State's Responsible Use of Generative AI Task Force is charged with developing university policy, guidelines, training and support services for student, faculty, researcher and staff use and procurement of generative AI tools.
initiatives.significance: Adds source-backed detail to the public AI posture.

investment.description: No public dollar amount disclosed for this initiative.

initiatives.title: Beocat HPC cluster AI-capable research infrastructure
initiatives.description: Beocat is K-State's high-performance computing cluster run by the Institute for Computational Research, supporting researchers statewide and providing a named compute-capacity signal even though current GPU counts are not public.

regulatory_context.summary: State and system AI context checked only at high level; institution-level sources carry the profile.

narrative_summary: Kansas State University shows an AI posture with five documented initiatives across 4 categories. The evidence emphasizes Responsible GenAI task force, Generative AI records guidance, and AI-focused computing pipeline. Policy maturity is coded as cautious based on public guidance, privacy language, governance, or tool controls. The profile also records teaching, research, workforce, equity, or enterprise-tool activity where sources support it. Leadership names, enrollment, GPU counts, and contract values are left null when not found in the cited public sources.

site:k-state.edu Kansas State University Beocat GPU high performance computing artificial intelligence
gaps_acknowledged: Leadership names and enrollment were not populated in this missing-only sweep.
Detailed AI budgets, user counts, and GPU counts were not consistently public.
No controversy record was added without a reliable institution-specific source.
Follow-up search closed compute_capacity at the named-system level with Beocat, but no current public GPU count was found.
Follow-up search found task-force and teaching-resource evidence but no public enterprise AI user counts, contract values, or AI-specific institutional investment total.
